[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space height=”50px”][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row el_class=”flexrow”][vc_column width=”1/4″ el_class=”flextwo gt-custom-sidebar-column”][vc_widget_sidebar sidebar_id=”honeymoon-package-international-sidebar”][/vc_column][vc_column width=”3/4″ el_class=”second-div-text flexone”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Italy offers the perfect setting for a romantic escapade with historical sites, beautiful sunsets, and breathtaking views. No wonder it is one of the most exotic and picturesque destinations in Europe. At ATW Travels, we have put together several Italy honeymoon packages that match your budget and travel style. Browse through a range of Italy honeymoon packages with exciting deals from ATW Travels.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Why is Italy the Perfect Place for Honeymoon?” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Italy is a dream destination for newlyweds looking to celebrate the start of a new life. The country offers jaw-dropping scenery, scrumptious food, mesmerizing architecture, and rich cultural heritage. Unlike other countries, Italy’s Mediterranean climate makes it a great destination all year round.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26242″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Must-See Destinations Included in Italy Honeymoon Packages” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Rome” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

The capital city of Italy is an integral part of all Italy honeymoon packages. It offers the best of Roman architecture and is home to the Vatican, St. Peter’s Basilica, the majestic Colosseum, Trevi Fountains, and the Pantheon. Rome was also the center of the Renaissance, thanks to the massive papal patronage. Some of the world’s greatest artists and creators like Michelangelo, Bernini, and Raphael once called this place home. Explore Rome with your loved one, walk down cobbled pathways, spend nights at the high-spirited town squares and piazzas, and experience the sheer beauty of this ancient town in all its glory.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Lake Como” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Lake Como is a breathtakingly beautiful resort area and the third largest lake in Italy. The place is frequented by celebrities from across the world thanks to picturesque Victorian gardens, Michelin-starred lakeshore restaurants, and charming resort villages. Of the five major lakes in Italy, Lake Como is the most popular.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Milan” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Milan has an elegant vibe that attracts designers, art lovers, and photographers from across the world. Unlike Rome, Venice, or Florence, it is less touristy but offers plenty of entertainment options for lovestruck honeymooners. Whether you’re visiting the Santa Maria delle Grazie church or shopping at the Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II, you are sure to enjoy the city like never before.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Tuscany” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

You would wonder if you have woken up in your dream when in Tuscany. With its picturesque countryside and vast expanses of vineyards, Tuscany is definitely the most beautiful city in Florence. This region boasts 7 UNESCO world heritage sites thanks to the wonderfully preserved architectural wonders, including Florence, Pisa, San Gimignano, and Siena. When in Tuscany, don’t forget to indulge in some wine tasting at Chianti or drive out to Umbria to spend a day away from the touristy hustle-bustle. Tuscany sure needs a place in your Italy honeymoon package.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Amalfi Coast” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

This sophisticated coastal belt consists of 13 beach towns with an elitist flavour to all aspects like food, restaurants, hotels, and people. The mountains and blue oceans reflect the heritage of the towns and are simply marvelous. The coast offers many hiking trails and has terrific food destinations. Amalfi Coast is home to Naples- the birthplace of Pizza, Capri-the glam island with beautiful beaches & boutiques, and more. You must visit the Amalfi Coast as part of your Italy honeymoon packages.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26245″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Sicily and Sardinia” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

These two exotic islands are home to beautiful beaches, sparkling blue waters, and exquisite food. Add them to your Italy honeymoon packages to experience peace and tranquillity.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Venice” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Venice is an archipelago of over 100 small islands built over the Adriatic Sea and connected with footbridges. There are no roads or cars anywhere in the city – you can explore the place on foot with your beloved and soak in the local flavours. You can even take a romantic gondola ride across the canals and catch a glimpse of the entire city. Add Venice to your Italy honeymoon package to experience this magical city.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”How to Get to Italy” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Italy is well connected and has plenty of international airports. Rome is the leading global entry point, followed by Milan. The main airports in the country include Pisa Galileo Galilei International Airport in Pisa, Leonardo da Vinci-Fiumicino International Airport in Rome, Malpensa International Airport in Milan, and Florence Amerigo Vespucci Airport in Florence, and Palermo International Airport in Sicily. All major airlines from across the world fly to most Italian cities.

You can also take a luxury cruise from Mumbai high seas to Rome as an extended vacation that takes 18 long days and touches five different countries. You can enter Italy by road if you travel from France, Austria, Switzerland, or Slovenia.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26246″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Transfers Within Italy” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Traveling by train is the fastest, most convenient way to get around Italy. Some regions like Tuscany must be explored in a car, while towns like Sicily, Sardinia, and Naples can be accessed by ferry. Yet another experience to add to your travelogue.

ATW Travels can arrange transfers throughout your honeymoon trip as a part of our Italy honeymoon packages.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner full_width=”stretch_row_content_no_spaces” css=”.vc_custom_1645675083319{margin-bottom: 20px !important;padding-top: 40px !important;padding-bottom: 40px !important;background-color: #1a2b48 !important;}” el_id=”mobileshoecontact” el_class=”show-mobile”][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1646220146083{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

[/vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What to Eat in Italy?” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Italy is famous worldwide for its culinary delight, with the cuisine tasting the best only in Italy. Here’s a list of some of the best Italian delicacies you must try when in the country:

Risotto

A rice-based dish cooked in cream, cheese, broth, onions, and other authentic Italian condiments.

Pizza

This is one of the most popular dishes in Italy made using fresh ingredients and cooked in a wood-fired oven.

Pasta

Pasta was introduced to Italians by the Arabs, although some believe the spaghetti was inspired by the Chinese noodles and brought to the country by Marco Polo. Today, there are over 350 varieties of pasta available in different shapes and sizes – it is believed that each shape and size serves a special purpose and go best with different sauces. When in Italy, you must try different pasta dishes, ranging from simple spaghetti Aglio e olio to complex pasta dishes made using exotic ingredients.

Gnocchi

Potato-based dumplings served with different sauces and toppings cheese, spinach, or pesto.

Gelato

It is a traditional dessert richer in flavor and consistency than regular ice-creams and is available in hundreds of flavors all over the country.

Pesto

A traditional sauce prepared with seven essential ingredients and ground using a classic wooden mortar and pestle. The pesto is most often used to fill pasta or spread on croutons.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26247″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”The Best Italy Honeymoon Tour Packages Include the Best Shopping Destinations” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Italy is the best place for souvenir shopping. Venetian glass and masks, leather goods from Florentine, hand-painted ceramics, and fashion ensembles- Italy has it all and more. You will find a few pieces you want to carry back home for keepsake or gifting. Each destination in Italy is famous for a few items, and there are some markets where you can find them.

  • Via Del Corso, Rome, for street shopping and souvenir shopping
  • Via Giovan Battista Zannoni, Florence for the best leather
  • Mercerie, Venice to buy luxury items, jewelry, etc.
  • Via Toledo, Naples to shop at one of the many high-end boutiques

Italy has something for everyone. For the best vacation experience, plan your honeymoon with ATW Travels. Our travel experts can customize Italy honeymoon packages that match your budget and style. Our meticulous planning ensures there’s little room for disappointments, and your honeymoon is the most memorable experience of your life. Get to know about our daily offers by visiting our Instagram Profile.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner el_class=”removepadding”][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]
